user.title: Set up Lightener
user.description: Create a Lightener group, optionally narrow by room, pick the lights, then open **Lightener Studio** to choose the first shape on the graph.

area.title: Filter by area (optional)
area.description: Select a room or area to narrow down the lights on the next screen. With 100+ lights this saves a lot of scrolling. Leave blank to see all lights.

lights.title: Select lights
lights.description: Pick the lights to control. You'll tune each light's brightness curve visually in the **Lightener Studio** in the next step — no number-crunching here.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"

create_entry.open_editor: Group created. **[Open Lightener Studio]({editor_url})** to choose a starting shape on the graph.

abort.open_editor: **[Continue in Lightener Studio →]({editor_url})** — pick lights, choose a curve preset, and shape the response on the live graph.

stale_domain_folder_collision.title: Two Lightener Studio folders are on disk
stale_domain_folder_collision.description:
Both `custom_components/lightener_studio/` and a stray `custom_components/lightener/` declare the same integration domain, so Home Assistant picks one unpredictably at startup — a restart can silently load the wrong version.

This happens when HACS still has the pre-rename domain (`lightener`) cached for this repository and extracts updates into the old folder.

To fix it:
1. Delete the `custom_components/lightener/` folder. If you are unsure which folder holds the newer build, check the linked guide first.
2. In HACS, remove the Lightener Studio custom repository, then add it again so HACS re-reads the current domain. Remove it from HACS only — do **not** remove the integration from Settings → Devices & Services, which would delete your configured groups and curves.
3. Redownload Lightener Studio in HACS, then restart Home Assistant.

stale_domain_folder_legacy.title: Leftover pre-rename Lightener folder found
stale_domain_folder_legacy.description:
A `custom_components/lightener/` folder from this integration's pre-rename days is still on disk. Home Assistant ignores it right now, but a future HACS update can extract into it and create a duplicate-domain conflict.

To clean it up:
1. Delete the `custom_components/lightener/` folder.
2. In HACS, remove and re-add the Lightener Studio repository so it re-reads the current domain (`lightener_studio`). Remove it from HACS only — do **not** remove the integration from Settings → Devices & Services, which would delete your configured groups and curves.
3. Restart Home Assistant.

init.title: Select lights
init.description: Add or remove lights here. Open **Lightener Studio** in the sidebar to pick presets and tune curves visually — new lights start with a linear curve.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"
